model_narrative: We plan how to mine a group of open pits over a sequence of time periods. Each pit is divided into segments stacked from top to bottom, and the segments of a pit have to be taken in order from the top down. In every period we decide which segments are available to extract, how far down the extraction has progressed in each pit, how much material to take from each segment, and the resulting output of each pit. Material taken from a segment earns a net benefit per unit that can be positive or negative, and value earned in later periods is discounted relative to earlier periods. The objective is to maximize the total discounted net income over the whole horizon.

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the set of pits that can be mined

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the set of extraction segments within a pit, listed in the fixed order in which they must be mined; the first member is the topmost segment and each later member lies deeper and can only be reached after the ones above it

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the set of extraction periods in chronological order; the first member is the opening period and each later member follows the one before it

params.name: nev
params.doc: the net extraction benefit earned per unit of material taken from a segment of a pit, in value per unit; it may be negative for segments whose material costs more to handle than it returns

params.name: evo
params.doc: the total volume of material contained in a segment of a pit, in units; this is the most that can ever be taken from that segment across the whole horizon

params.name: demand
params.doc: the product demand that must be met in each period, in units

params.name: delta
params.doc: the discount factor applied to value earned in each period, a dimensionless multiplier that weights earlier periods more than later ones

vars.name: b
vars.doc: equals 1 if a segment of a pit is available to be extracted in a period and 0 otherwise

vars.name: e
vars.doc: equals 1 if a segment is the last (deepest) segment whose extraction sequence ends in a period for that pit, and 0 otherwise

vars.name: open
vars.doc: equals 1 if a segment of a pit is ever activated for extraction over the whole horizon, and 0 otherwise

vars.name: ej
vars.doc: the position in the segment ordering of the last segment whose sequence ends in a period for a pit; bounded between 0 and the number of segments

vars.name: out
vars.doc: the amount of material extracted from a segment of a pit in a period, in units

vars.name: pout
vars.doc: the total material output of a pit in a period, in units; bounded above by eighty percent of that period's demand

vars.name: obj_var
vars.doc: the total discounted net income earned over the whole horizon, in value

constraints.name: eone
constraints.intent: In every period, each pit's extraction sequence must end at exactly one of its segments. For each pit and period, exactly one segment is marked as the one where the extraction sequence ends.

constraints.name: etwo
constraints.intent: For each pit and period, record how deep the extraction has reached by tying the ending position to whichever segment the sequence ends at. The ending position equals the place of that ending segment in the top-to-bottom ordering of the pit's segments.

constraints.name: ethree
constraints.intent: Within each pit the extraction can only progress downward over time and never move back up. For each pit, the ending position reached in a period must be at least as deep as the ending position reached in the period before it.

constraints.name: brun
constraints.intent: A segment of a pit becomes available to extract in a period according to how the extraction has unfolded above it and before it. For each pit, segment, and period, availability comes from the segment directly above it having been available but not yet where the sequence ended in that same period, plus that same segment having been where the sequence ended in the previous period. The topmost segment in the opening period is available from the start.

constraints.name: defpout
constraints.intent: The total output of a pit in a period is built up from what is extracted across its segments. For each pit and period, the pit's output equals the sum of the material taken from all of its segments in that period.

constraints.name: dem
constraints.intent: In every period the pits together must supply exactly the required amount. For each period, the outputs of all pits add up to that period's demand.

constraints.name: opendef
constraints.intent: A segment of a pit counts as activated whenever it is available to extract in any period. For each pit, segment, and period, the segment being available in that period forces it to be marked activated.

constraints.name: openlow
constraints.intent: A segment of a pit may be marked activated only if it is actually available to extract in at least one period. For each pit and segment, activation cannot exceed the number of periods in which the segment is available.

constraints.name: outlim
constraints.intent: Material can be taken from a segment in a period only if that segment is available then, and never more than the volume the segment holds. For each pit, segment, and period, the amount extracted is at most the segment's total volume when it is available, and is forced to zero when it is not.

constraints.name: outall
constraints.intent: A segment must be completely mined out before the segment directly below it in the same pit can be activated. For each pit and segment, the material taken from that segment across all periods must reach its full volume whenever the next deeper segment is activated. The deepest segment has nothing below it and so carries no such requirement.

constraints.name: outmax
constraints.intent: Over the whole horizon, no more than a segment's volume can be taken from it, and nothing can be taken unless the segment is activated. For each pit and segment, the material taken across all periods is at most the segment's volume when it is activated and zero otherwise.

constraints.name: defobj
constraints.intent: The total discounted net income accumulates the value of every bit of material extracted, weighted by its per-unit benefit and discounted by the period in which it is taken. Set the total income equal to the sum over all pits, segments, and periods of the discount factor times the per-unit net benefit times the amount extracted.

query: We're looking at a haul-fleet limit on pit p4 — its trucks can only move so much over the life of the plan. Suppose p4's total output across all four periods is held to 20 units. What does the optimizer come back with for discounted net income?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_p4 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.pout['p4', t] for t in model.t) <= 20)

query: There's an idea to keep pit p3 active rather than barely touched. If we require p3 to contribute at least 8 units of total output over the horizon, what discounted net income does the plan produce?
expected_pyomo: model.floor_p3 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.pout['p3', t] for t in model.t) >= 8)

query: Pit p3 barely gets used — it only opens 13 of its 50 segments and ships about 5 units all told. Surely it could carry more of the load; couldn't we lean on p3 for at least 10 units across the horizon? What's the model seeing that makes that a bad call?
expected_pyomo: model.why_p3 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.pout['p3', t] for t in model.t) >= 10)

query: The plan opens up every last one of pit p1's 50 segments — that's a lot of overburden stripping and activated benches. Couldn't we run a leaner footprint and hold p1 to at most 35 open segments? What's the model trading off by digging the whole column?
expected_pyomo: model.why_open_p1 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.open['p1', s] for s in model.s) <= 35)
